title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability sits at a severe 10% (42/190), B200 at 14% (99/190), A100 at 20% (96/311), and H200 at 21% (72/208). The headline move: H100 spot surged 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr while utilization hit 90.3%, up 10.5pp in 30 days, signaling a demand squeeze that is pulling B200 and H200 pricing higher in sympathy.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability sits at a severe 10% (42/190), B200 at 14% (99/190), A100 at 20% (96/311), and H200 at 21% (72/208). The headline move: H100 spot surged 42.9% day-over-day to $2.86/hr while utilization hit 90.3%, up 10.5pp in 30 days, signaling a demand squeeze that is pulling B200 and H200 pricing higher in sympathy.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs roughly $10.45/M tokens, meaning medium-tier providers charging $3/M are selling at a loss of about $7.45 per million tokens. DeepSeek and Meta-hosted Llama at $0.30-$0.60/M output are deep underwater on every GPU except possibly reserved-rate A100s. Grades: OpenAI Strong (premium tiers hold 90%+ margins on B200), Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 is undervalued at $4.99/hr spot given 14% availability and the best inference margin profile, with Llama 70B yielding 92% margin at premium tier. H200 at $4.00/hr with 121.7% annualized OCPI volatility and softening utilization (down 4.5pp) is overpriced relative to B200; rotate H200 exposure into B200 contracts.
bullets: 10% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all four classes.
14% of B200 GPUs are available, qualifying as a severe shortage.
$2.86 H100 spot median is up 42.9% in a single day.
21.76 LLMflation index means frontier output costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.06 per million output tokens for Llama 3.1 8B is the cheapest inference ever tracked.
90.3% H100 utilization is the highest reading in the dataset, up 10.5 percentage points over 30 days.
